frame_analyses.description: Close-up view of white braided suture with needle already loaded, appears to be mounted in trainer
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Suture appears to be silk or similar braided material, needle not clearly visible

frame_analyses.description: Hands positioning suture through what appears to be Penrose drain material, both hands working with suture
Right hand holding needle driver, left hand manipulating suture, working on drain placement
Both hands working extracorporeally to form knot, suture tails visible being manipulated
Continued knot formation, hands manipulating suture tails to create secure knot
Final positioning and tightening of knot, hands working to secure the construct
Final view showing completed knot and suture construct

suture_placement.deviation_assessment: Cannot determine exact deviation from marks - marks not clearly visible in video frames

confidence_rationale: Clear view of technique and timing, but Penrose drain marks and exact suture placement accuracy cannot be determined from the video angles provided. Camera angle and lighting make precise penalty assessment difficult.

Precise gap measurement in drain - insufficient close-up detail
Needle placement accuracy relative to target marks
technique_summary: Excellent expert-level performance with total time of 65 seconds. Demonstrates proper extracorporeal knot tying technique with good bimanual coordination throughout.
strengths: Fast completion time (65s vs 60-100s expert benchmark)
Good instrument handling and bimanual coordination
improvement_suggestions: Video quality limitations prevent identification of specific technical improvements
Performance appears to be at expert level based on observable metrics
